title: Wedding Photo Checklist 2026: Don't Miss a Single Moment
description: The complete photo checklist for 2026 weddings — from prep to party. What the photographer and QR code need to capture.
author: Guestcam Team
tldr: Before the wedding, prep the shot list and QR-code gallery. During the event the photographer captures the set pieces, guests with the QR catch the unexpected.

content.text: Before the wedding: what to prepare?

items: Book the wedding photographer at least 8-12 months in advance.
Prepare a 'must-have' shot list (ceremony, rings, first dance).
Create your Guestcam gallery 1-2 weeks before the wedding.
Print QR cards for every table — test that the code works.
Tell guests about the gallery in the wedding invitation.

content.alt: Wedding details: rings and the bride's bouquet
content.caption: Details the photographer might miss — guests almost never do.

content.text: During the ceremony: what shots do you need?

items: Arrival of bride and groom (separately).
The rings — close-up and from the audience.
First kiss.
Signing at the registrar.
Family group photos (plan the order).

content.alt: Wedding ceremony — bride and groom at the altar
content.caption: Every key moment — from the vows to the first dance — belongs on the list.

content.text: During the reception: which moments do guests capture better?
The photographer watches the ceremony. Guests watch the dance floor. Encourage guests to capture:

items: The dance floor — best laughter happens here.
Kids at the wedding — the photographer often misses them.
Grandparents watching the first dance.
The friend group in candid moments.
Table details, decoration, guest outfits.

content.text: After the wedding: how do you collect and organise everything?

items: In the week after the event, send guests a reminder about the gallery.
Download all photos from the Guestcam gallery (ZIP).
Merge with the photographer's deliverables (Premium supports direct photographer uploads too).
Back up on an external drive or cloud.
Order the wedding album — Guestcam exports a print-ready ZIP.

content.text: Move all the points above onto a single A4 page, print it and hand it to the photographer a week before. A lot less stress on the day.
Set up your gallery for the 2026 wedding

content.q: When should I book a photographer for a 2026 wedding?
content.a: The best photographers are booked 8-12 months out. For summer 2026, start the search now — popular dates (June, September) fill up fast.

content.q: How many photos should I expect from the photographer?
content.a: On average 300-600 edited photos for an 8-hour wedding. Add Guestcam and you get another 200-400 from guests.

content.q: Do the photographer and Guestcam work together?
content.a: Yes. The Premium plan lets the photographer upload their raw set to the same gallery (with the couple's agreement). All memories in one place.

content.q: What's the ideal time of day for outdoor photos?
content.a: Golden hour — about 1 hour before sunset. In Central Europe in June, that's around 20:00-21:00. Plan the ceremony schedule around that.

content.q: What if a guest doesn't want to be in photos?
content.a: Enable moderation in the Guestcam gallery — delete or hide unwanted shots. Let everyone know the gallery is private and guests can opt out at any time.
